This poses two central challenges: how scenarios are extracted and how they are grouped. Existing extraction methods rely on heterogeneous definitions, hindering scenario comparability. For the grouping of scenarios, rule-based or ML-based methods can be utilized. However, while modern ML-based approaches can handle the complexity of traffic scenarios, unlike rule-based approaches, they lack interpretability and may not align with domain-knowledge. This work contributes to a standardized scenario extraction based on the Scenario-as-Specification concept, as well as a domain-knowledge-guided scenario clustering process. Experiments on the highD dataset demonstrate that scenarios can be extracted reliably and that domain-knowledge can be effectively integrated into the clustering process. As a result, the proposed methodology supports a more standardized process for deriving scenario categories from highway data recordings and thus enables a more efficient validation process of automated vehicles.
tldr: This paper introduces a standardized, behavior-centric approach for extracting and clustering traffic scenarios from highway data, using a CVQ-VAE model guided by domain knowledge, to improve the validation of autonomous driving systems.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种标准化的、以行为为中心的交通场景提取和聚类方法，利用领域知识指导的 CVQ-VAE 模型从高速公路数据中提取并聚类交通场景，从而改进自动驾驶系统的验证。
summary_zh: 无人驾驶系统（ADS）的批准取决于评估其在具有代表性的真实交通场景中的行为。获取此类场景的常用方法是从真实世界的数据记录中提取。这些场景随后可以被分组，并作为ADS后续测试的基础。这带来了两个核心挑战：如何提取场景以及如何对场景进行分组。现有的提取方法依赖于异构的定义，阻碍了场景的可比性。对于场景的分组，可以使用基于规则或基于机器学习的方法。然而，虽然现代基于机器学习的方法可以处理交通场景的复杂性，但与基于规则的方法不同，它们缺乏可解释性，并且可能与领域知识不一致。本工作致力于基于情景即规范（Scenario-as-Specification）概念的标准化情景提取，以及一个领域知识指导的情景聚类过程。在highD数据集上的实验表明，情景可以被可靠地提取，并且领域知识可以有效地整合到聚类过程中。因此，所提出的方法支持从高速公路数据记录中导出情景类别的一个更为标准化的过程，从而能够更有效地验证自动驾驶车辆。

title: Point-to-Mask: From Arbitrary Point Annotations to Mask-Level Infrared Small Target Detection
summary: Infrared small target detection (IRSTD) methods predominantly formulate the task as pixel-level segmentation, which requires costly dense annotations and is not well suited to tiny targets with weak texture and ambiguous boundaries. To address this issue, we propose Point-to-Mask, a framework that bridges low-cost point supervision and mask-level detection through two components: a Physics-driven Adaptive Mask Generation (PAMG) module that converts point annotations into compact target masks and geometric cues, and a lightweight Radius-aware Point Regression Network (RPR-Net) that reformulates IRSTD as target center localization and effective radius regression using spatiotemporal motion cues. The two modules form a closed loop: PAMG generates pseudo masks and geometric supervision during training, while the geometric predictions of RPR-Net are fed back to PAMG for pixel-level mask recovery during inference. To facilitate systematic evaluation, we further construct SIRSTD-Pixel, a sequential dataset with refined pixel-level annotations. Experiments show that the proposed framework achieves strong pseudo-label quality, high detection accuracy, and efficient inference, approaching full-supervision performance under point-supervised settings with substantially lower annotation cost. Code and datasets will be available at: https://github.com/GaoScience/point-to-mask.
tldr: The paper introduces Point-to-Mask, a novel framework for infrared small target detection (IRSTD) that uses point annotations instead of costly pixel-level segmentation, achieving comparable performance to full supervision with significantly lower annotation costs.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个名为Point-to-Mask的新框架，用于红外小目标检测（IRSTD），它使用点注释而不是昂贵的像素级分割，并且在显着降低注释成本的情况下实现了与完全监督相当的性能。
summary_zh: 红外小目标检测(IRSTD)方法主要将任务形式化为像素级分割，这需要昂贵的密集标注，且不适用于具有微弱纹理和模糊边界的微小目标。为了解决这个问题，我们提出Point-to-Mask框架，通过两个组件桥接低成本的点监督和掩码级检测：一个物理驱动的自适应掩码生成(PAMG)模块，将点标注转换为紧凑的目标掩码和几何线索；以及一个轻量级的半径感知点回归网络(RPR-Net)，利用时空运动线索将IRSTD重新定义为目标中心定位和有效半径回归。这两个模块形成一个闭环：PAMG在训练期间生成伪掩码和几何监督，而RPR-Net的几何预测被反馈回PAMG，用于在推理期间进行像素级掩码恢复。为了方便系统评估，我们进一步构建了SIRSTD-Pixel，一个具有精细像素级标注的序列数据集。实验表明，所提出的框架实现了强大的伪标签质量、高检测精度和高效的推理，在点监督设置下以大大降低的标注成本接近了全监督的性能。代码和数据集将在https://github.com/GaoScience/point-to-mask上提供。

title: Structured prototype regularization for synthetic-to-real driving scene parsing
summary: Driving scene parsing is critical for autonomous vehicles to operate reliably in complex real-world traffic environments. To reduce the reliance on costly pixel-level annotations, synthetic datasets with automatically generated labels have become a popular alternative. However, models trained on synthetic data often perform poorly when applied to real-world scenes due to the synthetic-to-real domain gap. Despite the success of unsupervised domain adaptation in narrowing this gap, most existing methods mainly focus on global feature alignment while overlooking the semantic structure of the feature space. As a result, semantic relations among classes are insufficiently modeled, limiting the model's ability to generalize. To address these challenges, this study introduces a novel unsupervised domain adaptation framework that explicitly regularizes semantic feature structures to significantly enhance driving scene parsing performance in real-world scenarios. Specifically, the proposed method enforces inter-class separation and intra-class compactness by leveraging class-specific prototypes, thereby enhancing the discriminability and structural coherence of feature clusters. An entropy-based noise filtering strategy improves the reliability of pseudo labels, while a pixel-level attention mechanism further refines feature alignment. Extensive experiments on representative benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms recent state-of-the-art methods. These results underscore the importance of preserving semantic structure for robust synthetic-to-real adaptation in driving scene parsing tasks.
tldr: This paper presents a novel unsupervised domain adaptation framework for driving scene parsing, using structured prototype regularization to improve the generalization of models trained on synthetic data to real-world scenes.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种新颖的无监督领域自适应框架，用于驾驶场景解析，通过使用结构化的原型正则化来提高在合成数据上训练的模型在真实场景中的泛化能力。
summary_zh: 驾驶场景解析对于自动驾驶车辆在复杂的真实交通环境中可靠运行至关重要。为了减少对昂贵的像素级标注的依赖，具有自动生成标签的合成数据集已成为一种流行的替代方案。然而，由于合成到真实世界的领域差距，在合成数据上训练的模型在应用于真实场景时通常表现不佳。尽管无监督领域自适应在缩小这一差距方面取得了成功，但现有的大多数方法主要集中于全局特征对齐，而忽略了特征空间的语义结构。因此，类别之间的语义关系建模不足，限制了模型的泛化能力。为了应对这些挑战，本研究提出了一种新的无监督领域自适应框架，该框架明确地正则化语义特征结构，以显著提高真实场景中的驾驶场景解析性能。具体而言，所提出的方法通过利用类别特定的原型来加强类间分离和类内紧凑性，从而增强特征聚类的可区分性和结构一致性。一种基于熵的噪声过滤策略提高了伪标签的可靠性，而像素级注意力机制进一步优化了特征对齐。在具有代表性的基准数据集上进行的大量实验表明，所提出的方法始终优于最新的方法。这些结果强调了在驾驶场景解析任务中，保持语义结构对于鲁棒的合成到真实世界自适应的重要性。

title: ModTrack: Sensor-Agnostic Multi-View Tracking via Identity-Informed PHD Filtering with Covariance Propagation
summary: Multi-View Multi-Object Tracking (MV-MOT) aims to localize and maintain consistent identities of objects observed by multiple sensors. This task is challenging, as viewpoint changes and occlusion disrupt identity consistency across views and time. Recent end-to-end approaches address this by jointly learning 2D Bird's Eye View (BEV) representations and identity associations, achieving high tracking accuracy. However, these methods offer no principled uncertainty accounting and remain tightly coupled to their training configuration, limiting generalization across sensor layouts, modalities, or datasets without retraining. We propose ModTrack, a modular MV-MOT system that matches end-to-end performance while providing cross-modal, sensor-agnostic generalization and traceable uncertainty. ModTrack confines learning methods to just the \textit{Detection and Feature Extraction} stage of the MV-MOT pipeline, performing all fusion, association, and tracking with closed-form analytical methods. Our design reduces each sensor's output to calibrated position-covariance pairs $(\mathbf{z}, R)$; cross-view clustering and precision-weighted fusion then yield unified estimates $(\hat{\mathbf{z}}, \hat{R})$ for identity assignment and temporal tracking. A feedback-coupled, identity-informed Gaussian Mixture Probability Hypothesis Density (GM-PHD) filter with HMM motion modes uses these fused estimates to maintain identities under missed detections and heavy occlusion. ModTrack achieves 95.5 IDF1 and 91.4 MOTA on \textit{WildTrack}, surpassing all prior modular methods by over 21 points and rivaling the state-of-the-art end-to-end methods while providing deployment flexibility they cannot. Specifically, the same tracker core transfers unchanged to \textit{MultiviewX} and \textit{RadarScenes}, with only perception-module replacement required to extend to new domains and sensor modalities.
tldr: ModTrack is a modular, sensor-agnostic Multi-View Multi-Object Tracking (MV-MOT) system that achieves state-of-the-art performance by performing fusion, association, and tracking with closed-form analytical methods and an identity-informed Gaussian Mixture Probability Hypothesis Density filter.
tldr_zh: ModTrack是一个模块化的、传感器无关的多视角多目标跟踪（MV-MOT）系统，通过使用闭式解析方法和身份感知的Gaussian Mixture Probability Hypothesis Density滤波器进行融合、关联和跟踪，实现了最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 多视角多目标跟踪 (MV-MOT) 旨在定位并保持多个传感器观测到的对象的一致身份。这项任务具有挑战性，因为视角变化和遮挡会扰乱跨视角和时间段的身份一致性。最近的端到端方法通过联合学习二维鸟瞰图 (BEV) 表示和身份关联来解决这个问题，实现了高跟踪精度。然而，这些方法没有提供有原则的不确定性计算，并且仍然与其训练配置紧密耦合，限制了在没有重新训练的情况下跨传感器布局、模态或数据集的泛化能力。我们提出了 ModTrack，一个模块化的 MV-MOT 系统，它在提供跨模态、传感器无关的泛化和可追溯的不确定性的同时，匹配了端到端性能。ModTrack 将学习方法限制在 MV-MOT 流程的 \textit{检测和特征提取} 阶段，并使用闭式解析方法执行所有融合、关联和跟踪。我们的设计将每个传感器的输出简化为经过校准的位置-协方差对 $(\mathbf{z}, R)$；跨视角聚类和精度加权融合随后产生统一的估计 $(\hat{\mathbf{z}}, \hat{R})$，用于身份分配和时间跟踪。一个反馈耦合的、身份感知的具有 HMM 运动模式的高斯混合概率假设密度 (GM-PHD) 滤波器使用这些融合估计来维护在漏检和严重遮挡下的身份。ModTrack 在 \textit{WildTrack} 上实现了 95.5 的 IDF1 和 91.4 的 MOTA，超过了所有先前的模块化方法 21 多个点，并且与最先进的端到端方法相媲美，同时提供了它们无法提供的部署灵活性。具体来说，相同的跟踪器核心无需修改即可转移到 \textit{MultiviewX} 和 \textit{RadarScenes}，只需更换感知模块即可扩展到新的领域和传感器模态。

title: Conservative Continuous-Time Treatment Optimization
summary: We develop a conservative continuous-time stochastic control framework for treatment optimization from irregularly sampled patient trajectories. The unknown patient dynamics are modeled as a controlled stochastic differential equation with treatment as a continuous-time control. Naive model-based optimization can exploit model errors and propose out-of-support controls, so optimizing the estimated dynamics may not optimize the true dynamics. To limit extrapolation, we add a consistent signature-based MMD regularizer on path space that penalizes treatment plans whose induced trajectory distribution deviates from observed trajectories. The resulting objective minimizes a computable upper bound on the true cost. Experiments on benchmark datasets show improved robustness and performance compared to non-conservative baselines.
tldr: The paper proposes a conservative continuous-time stochastic control framework for treatment optimization, using a signature-based MMD regularizer to prevent extrapolation and improve robustness in treatment planning.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种保守的连续时间随机控制框架，用于治疗优化。它使用基于签名的MMD正则化器来防止外推，并提高治疗计划的鲁棒性。
summary_zh: 我们开发了一种保守的连续时间随机控制框架，用于从不规则采样的患者轨迹中优化治疗方案。未知的患者动态被建模为受控随机微分方程，治疗方案作为连续时间控制。 朴素的基于模型的优化可能会利用模型误差并提出超出支持范围的控制方案，因此优化估计的动态可能无法优化真实的动态。 为了限制外推，我们在路径空间中添加了一个基于签名的一致性MMD正则化项，该正则化项惩罚那些诱导的轨迹分布偏离观测轨迹的治疗计划。 最终的目标是最小化真实成本的可计算上界。 在基准数据集上的实验表明，与非保守的基线方法相比，该方法具有更高的鲁棒性和性能。

title: Novelty-Driven Target-Space Discovery in Automated Electron and Scanning Probe Microscopy
summary: Modern automated microscopy faces a fundamental discovery challenge: in many systems, the most important scientific information does not reside in the immediately visible image features, but in the target space of sequentially acquired spectra or functional responses, making it essential to develop strategies that can actively search for new behaviors rather than simply optimize known objectives. Here, we developed a deep-kernel-learning BEACON framework that is explicitly designed to guide discovery in the target space by learning structure-property relationships during the experiment and using that evolving model to seek diverse response regimes. We first established the method through demonstration workflows built on pre-acquired ground-truth datasets, which enabled direct benchmarking against classical acquisition strategies and allowed us to define a set of monitoring functions for comparing exploration quality, target-space coverage, and surrogate-model behavior in a transparent and reproducible manner. This benchmarking framework provides a practical basis for evaluating discovery-driven algorithms, not just optimization performance. We then operationalized and deployed the workflow on STEM, showing that the approach can transition from offline validation to real experimental implementation. To support adoption and extension by the broader community, the associated notebooks are available, allowing users to reproduce the workflows, test the benchmarks, and adapt the method to their own instruments and datasets.
tldr: This paper introduces a deep-kernel-learning framework (BEACON) for automated microscopy that actively searches for new behaviors in the target space of spectra or functional responses, validated through benchmarked workflows and real experimental implementation on STEM.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种用于自动化显微镜的深度核学习框架 (BEACON)，该框架主动搜索光谱或功能响应目标空间中的新行为，并通过基准工作流程和 STEM 上的实际实验实施进行验证。
summary_zh: 现代自动化显微镜面临着一个根本性的发现挑战：在许多系统中，最重要的科学信息并非存在于直接可见的图像特征中，而是存在于顺序采集的光谱或功能响应的目标空间中，这使得开发能够主动搜索新行为而非仅仅优化已知目标的策略至关重要。在此，我们开发了一种深度核学习BEACON框架，其明确设计旨在通过学习实验期间的结构-属性关系并利用不断演化的模型来探寻多样化的响应机制，从而引导在目标空间中的发现过程。我们首先通过构建在预先获取的真值数据集之上的演示工作流程确立了该方法，这使得我们能够直接与经典的采集策略进行基准测试，并能够定义一组监测函数，用于以透明且可复现的方式比较探索质量、目标空间覆盖率以及替代模型行为。该基准测试框架为评估发现驱动的算法提供了实际基础，而不仅仅是优化性能。然后，我们在STEM上实施并部署了该工作流程，表明该方法可以从离线验证过渡到真实的实验实施。为了支持更广泛的社区采用和扩展，我们提供了相关的notebook，使用户能够复现这些工作流程，测试基准，并将该方法应用于其自己的仪器和数据集。

title: Trajectory-Optimized Time Reparameterization for Learning-Compatible Reduced-Order Modeling of Stiff Dynamical Systems
summary: Stiff dynamical systems present a challenge for machine-learning reduced-order models (ML-ROMs), as explicit time integration becomes unstable in stiff regimes while implicit integration within learning loops is computationally expensive and often degrades training efficiency. Time reparameterization (TR) offers an alternative by transforming the independent variable so that rapid physical-time transients are spread over a stretched-time coordinate, enabling stable explicit integration on uniformly sampled grids. Although several TR strategies have been proposed, their effect on learnability in ML-ROMs remains incompletely understood. This work investigates time reparameterization as a stiffness-mitigation mechanism for neural ODE reduced-order modeling and introduces a trajectory-optimized TR (TOTR) formulation. The proposed approach casts time reparameterization as an optimization problem in arc-length coordinates, in which a traversal-speed profile is selected to penalize acceleration in stretched time. By targeting the smoothness of the training dynamics, this formulation produces reparameterized trajectories that are better conditioned and easier to learn than existing TR methods. TOTR is evaluated on three stiff problems: a parameterized stiff linear system, the van der Pol oscillator, and the HIRES chemical kinetics model. Across all cases, the proposed approach yields smoother reparameterizations and improved physical-time predictions under identical training regimens than other TR approaches. Quantitative results demonstrate loss reductions of one to two orders of magnitude compared to benchmark algorithms. These results highlight that effective stiffness mitigation in ML-ROMs depends critically on the regularity and learnability of the time map itself, and that optimization-based TR provides a robust framework for explicit reduced-order modeling of multiscale dynamical systems.
tldr: This paper introduces a trajectory-optimized time reparameterization (TOTR) method for training machine learning reduced-order models (ML-ROMs) of stiff dynamical systems, showing improved smoothness and learnability compared to existing methods, leading to significant loss reductions.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种轨迹优化时间重参数化（TOTR）方法，用于训练刚性动力系统的机器学习降阶模型（ML-ROMs），与现有方法相比，该方法表现出更好的平滑性和可学习性，从而显著降低了损失。
summary_zh: 刚性动力系统对机器学习降阶模型(ML-ROM)提出了挑战，因为显式时间积分在刚性区域变得不稳定，而在学习循环中进行隐式积分计算成本高昂，并且常常降低训练效率。时间重参数化(TR)提供了一种替代方案，通过变换自变量，使得快速的物理时间瞬态能够在拉伸的时间坐标上展开，从而能够在均匀采样的网格上进行稳定的显式积分。虽然已经提出了几种TR策略，但它们对ML-ROM中可学习性的影响仍然不完全清楚。这项工作研究了时间重参数化作为神经ODE降阶模型的一种刚度缓解机制，并引入了一种轨迹优化TR(TOTR)公式。所提出的方法将时间重参数化转化为弧长坐标中的一个优化问题，在该问题中，选择一个遍历速度剖面来惩罚拉伸时间中的加速度。通过针对训练动力学的平滑性，这种公式产生了比现有TR方法更好条件且更容易学习的重参数化轨迹。TOTR在三个剛性问题上进行了评估：参数化剛性线性系统、范德波尔振荡器和HIRES化学动力学模型。在所有情况下，与其它TR方法相比，所提出的方法在相同的训练方案下，能够产生更平滑的重参数化和改进的物理时间预测。定量结果表明，与基准算法相比，损失降低了一到两个数量级。这些结果突出表明，ML-ROM中有效的刚度缓解关键取决于时间映射本身的规律性和可学习性，并且基于优化的TR为多尺度动力系统的显式降阶建模提供了一个稳健的框架。

title: Optimal uncertainty bounds for multivariate kernel regression under bounded noise: A Gaussian process-based dual function
summary: Non-conservative uncertainty bounds are essential for making reliable predictions about latent functions from noisy data--and thus, a key enabler for safe learning-based control. In this domain, kernel methods such as Gaussian process regression are established techniques, thanks to their inherent uncertainty quantification mechanism. Still, existing bounds either pose strong assumptions on the underlying noise distribution, are conservative, do not scale well in the multi-output case, or are difficult to integrate into downstream tasks. This paper addresses these limitations by presenting a tight, distribution-free bound for multi-output kernel-based estimates. It is obtained through an unconstrained, duality-based formulation, which shares the same structure of classic Gaussian process confidence bounds and can thus be straightforwardly integrated into downstream optimization pipelines. We show that the proposed bound generalizes many existing results and illustrate its application using an example inspired by quadrotor dynamics learning.
tldr: This paper presents a tight, distribution-free uncertainty bound for multi-output kernel regression, leveraging a duality-based formulation similar to Gaussian process bounds for easier integration into downstream tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种适用于多输出核回归的、紧致的、无分布的置信界限，利用了一种基于对偶性的公式，该公式与高斯过程置信界限的结构相似，便于集成到下游任务中。
summary_zh: 非保守的不确定性边界对于从噪声数据中可靠地预测潜在函数至关重要，因此是安全学习控制的关键赋能者。在该领域，诸如高斯过程回归等核方法由于其固有的不确定性量化机制而成为成熟的技术。然而，现有的边界要么对底层噪声分布提出了严格的假设，要么过于保守，要么在多输出情况下扩展性不佳，要么难以集成到下游任务中。本文通过提出一种针对多输出核估计的紧致的、无分布的边界来解决这些局限性。该边界通过无约束的、基于对偶的公式获得，该公式与经典高斯过程置信边界具有相同结构，因此可以方便地集成到下游优化流程中。我们证明了所提出的边界推广了许多现有的结果，并使用一个受四旋翼飞行器动力学学习启发的示例来说明其应用。

title: Deep Adaptive Model-Based Design of Experiments
summary: Model-based design of experiments (MBDOE) is essential for efficient parameter estimation in nonlinear dynamical systems. However, conventional adaptive MBDOE requires costly posterior inference and design optimization between each experimental step, precluding real-time applications. We address this by combining Deep Adaptive Design (DAD), which amortizes sequential design into a neural network policy trained offline, with differentiable mechanistic models. For dynamical systems with known governing equations but uncertain parameters, we extend sequential contrastive training objectives to handle nuisance parameters and propose a transformer-based policy architecture that respects the temporal structure of dynamical systems. We demonstrate the approach on four systems of increasing complexity: a fed-batch bioreactor with Monod kinetics, a Haldane bioreactor with uncertain substrate inhibition, a two-compartment pharmacokinetic model with nuisance clearance parameters, and a DC motor for real-time deployment.
tldr: This paper presents a method combining Deep Adaptive Design with differentiable mechanistic models for efficient parameter estimation in nonlinear dynamical systems, addressing the limitations of traditional model-based design of experiments, and demonstrates this approach on four systems with increasing complexity.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种将深度自适应设计与可微机械模型相结合的方法，用于非线性动力系统中高效的参数估计，解决了传统模型实验设计方法的局限性，并在四个复杂度递增的系统中演示了该方法。
summary_zh: 基于模型的实验设计（MBDOE）对于非线性动力系统中的高效参数估计至关重要。然而，传统的自适应MBDOE需要在每个实验步骤之间进行代价高昂的后验推断和设计优化，这阻碍了其实时应用。我们通过结合深度自适应设计（DAD）来解决这个问题，DAD将顺序设计摊销到离线训练的神经网络策略中，并结合可微分的机理模型。对于具有已知控制方程但参数不确定的动力系统，我们扩展了顺序对比训练目标以处理干扰参数，并提出了一种基于Transformer的策略架构，该架构遵循动力系统的时间结构。我们在四个复杂度递增的系统上验证了该方法：具有Monod动力学的分批补料生物反应器、具有不确定底物抑制的Haldane生物反应器、具有干扰清除参数的双室药代动力学模型，以及一个用于实时部署的直流电机。

title: Development of Low-Cost and Bidirectional Syringe Pumps for Soft Robotics Applications
summary: Soft robotics leverages deformable materials to develop robots capable of navigating unstructured and dynamic environments. Silicone Voxel-Based Soft Robots (Silibots) are a type of pneumatically actuated soft robots that rely on the inflation and deflation of their voxels for shape-shifting behaviors. However, traditional pneumatic actuation methods (high pressure solenoids, medical diaphragm pumps, micro compressors, compressed fluid) pose significant challenges due to their limited efficacy, cost, complexity, or lack of precision. This work introduces a low cost and modular syringe pump system, constructed with off the shelf and 3D printed parts, designed to overcome these limitations. The syringe pump system also enhances actuation with the unique ability to pull a vacuum as well pump air into the soft robot. Furthermore, the syringe pump features modular hardware and customizable software, allowing for researchers to tailor the syringe pump to their requirements or operate multiple pumps simultaneously with unique pump parameters. This flexibility makes the syringe pump an accessible and scalable tool that paves the way for broader adoption of soft robotic technologies in research and education.
tldr: This paper introduces a low-cost, bidirectional syringe pump system for actuating soft robots, addressing limitations of traditional pneumatic actuation methods.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种低成本、双向注射泵系统，用于驱动软体机器人，解决了传统气动驱动方法的局限性。
summary_zh: 软体机器人利用可变形材料开发能够在非结构化和动态环境中导航的机器人。基于硅胶体素的软体机器人（Silibots）是一种气动驱动的软体机器人，其形变行为依赖于体素的充气和放气。然而，传统的气动驱动方法（高压电磁阀、医用隔膜泵、微型压缩机、压缩流体）因其功效有限、成本高昂、复杂性高或缺乏精度而面临重大挑战。本研究提出了一种低成本且模块化的注射泵系统，该系统由现成的零件和3D打印的部件构成，旨在克服上述局限性。该注射泵系统还通过其独特的能力来增强驱动，既可以将空气泵入软体机器人，也可以对其进行抽真空。此外，注射泵具有模块化的硬件和可定制的软件，允许研究人员根据自身的需求定制注射泵或使用独特的泵参数同时操作多个泵。这种灵活性使得该注射泵成为一种易于获得且可扩展的工具，为软体机器人技术在研究和教育中的更广泛应用铺平了道路。

title: Thermopneumatic Pixels for Fast, Localized, Low-Voltage Touch Feedback
summary: We present thermopneumatic pixels (TPPs), which are tactile actuators designed for rapid fabrication and straightforward integration into compact wearable and surface-based haptic systems. Each TPP converts low-voltage ($\sim$10 V) electrical pulses into transient pressure increases within a sealed cavity, producing out-of-plane forces and displacements suitable for tactile stimulation. The architecture enables scalable fabrication and spatially distributed actuation while maintaining simple electrical interfacing. The TPPs are constructed from inexpensive, readily available materials using straightforward layer-based assembly, facilitating rapid prototyping and integration into interactive devices. Mechanical characterization demonstrates peak forces exceeding 1 N and millimeter displacements. We further present driving electronics for operating multiple TPP modules concurrently and report perceptual study results demonstrating the effectiveness of the resulting tactile feedback. Together, these results establish low-voltage thermopneumatic actuation as an accessible and high-performance approach for embedding tactile feedback into experimental and consumer-facing interfaces.
tldr: The paper introduces thermopneumatic pixels (TPPs), a low-voltage, rapidly fabricated, and easily integrated tactile actuator capable of producing significant force and displacement, shown to be effective in perceptual studies.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种热气动像素（TPP），这是一种低电压、快速制造且易于集成的触觉致动器，能够产生显著的力和位移，并在感知研究中显示出有效性。
summary_zh: 我们提出了一种热气动像素（TPP），这是一种触觉致动器，旨在用于快速制造并能直接集成到紧凑型可穿戴和表面触觉系统中。每个TPP将低压（约10伏）电脉冲转换为密封腔体内的瞬时压力升高，从而产生适用于触觉刺激的平面外力和位移。该架构实现了可扩展的制造和空间分布的驱动，同时保持了简单的电气接口。TPP采用廉价且易于获得的材料，通过简单的基于层的组装方式构建，从而促进了快速原型设计和集成到交互式设备中。机械特性表明其峰值力超过1牛顿，位移达毫米级。我们进一步介绍了用于同时操作多个TPP模块的驱动电子设备，并报告了感知研究结果，证明了由此产生的触觉反馈的有效性。总而言之，这些结果确立了低压热气动驱动作为一种易于使用且高性能的方法，可将触觉反馈嵌入到实验性和面向消费者的界面中。

title: ASCENT: Transformer-Based Aircraft Trajectory Prediction in Non-Towered Terminal Airspace
summary: Accurate trajectory prediction can improve General Aviation safety in non-towered terminal airspace, where high traffic density increases accident risk. We present ASCENT, a lightweight transformer-based model for multi-modal 3D aircraft trajectory forecasting, which integrates domain-aware 3D coordinate normalization and parameterized predictions. ASCENT employs a transformer-based motion encoder and a query-based decoder, enabling the generation of diverse maneuver hypotheses with low latency. Experiments on the TrajAir and TartanAviation datasets demonstrate that our model outperforms prior baselines, as the encoder effectively captures motion dynamics and the decoder aligns with structured aircraft traffic patterns. Furthermore, ablation studies confirm the contributions of the decoder design, coordinate-frame modeling, and parameterized outputs. These results establish ASCENT as an effective approach for real-time aircraft trajectory prediction in non-towered terminal airspace.
tldr: The paper introduces ASCENT, a transformer-based model for predicting aircraft trajectories in non-towered terminal airspace, demonstrating improved performance over existing methods by effectively capturing motion dynamics and aligning with traffic patterns.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了ASCENT，一种基于Transformer的模型，用于预测非塔台终端空域的飞机轨迹。通过有效地捕捉运动动力学并与交通模式对齐，该模型表现出优于现有方法的性能。
summary_zh: 精确的轨迹预测能够提升非塔台管制终端空域中的通用航空安全，在该空域中高流量密度增加了事故风险。我们提出了ASCENT，一种轻量级的基于Transformer的多模态3D飞机轨迹预测模型，该模型集成了领域感知的3D坐标归一化和参数化预测。 ASCENT采用基于Transformer的运动编码器和基于查询的解码器，能够以低延迟生成多样化的机动假设。在TrajAir和TartanAviation数据集上的实验表明，我们的模型优于之前的基准模型，因为编码器有效地捕捉了运动动力学，而解码器与结构化飞机交通模式对齐。此外，消融研究证实了解码器设计、坐标系建模和参数化输出的贡献。这些结果证明了ASCENT是一种用于非塔台管制终端空域中实时飞机轨迹预测的有效方法。

title: Early-Terminable Energy-Safe Iterative Coupling for Parallel Simulation of Port-Hamiltonian Systems
summary: Parallel simulation and control of large-scale robotic systems often rely on partitioned time stepping, yet finite-iteration coupling can inject spurious energy by violating power consistency--even when each subsystem is passive. This letter proposes a novel energy-safe, early-terminable iterative coupling for port-Hamiltonian subsystems by embedding a Douglas--Rachford (DR) splitting scheme in scattering (wave) coordinates. The lossless interconnection is enforced as an orthogonal constraint in the wave domain, while each subsystem contributes a discrete-time scattering port map induced by its one-step integrator. Under a discrete passivity condition on the subsystem time steps and a mild impedance-tuning condition, we prove an augmented-storage inequality certifying discrete passivity of the coupled macro-step for any finite inner-iteration budget, with the remaining mismatch captured by an explicit residual. As the inner budget increases, the partitioned update converges to the monolithic discrete-time update induced by the same integrators, yielding a principled, adaptive accuracy--compute trade-off, supporting energy-consistent real-time parallel simulation under varying computational budgets. Experiments on a coupled-oscillator benchmark validate the passivity certificates at numerical roundoff (on the order of 10e-14 in double precision) and show that the reported RMS state error decays monotonically with increasing inner-iteration budgets, consistent with the hard-coupling limit.
tldr: This paper presents an energy-safe, early-terminable iterative coupling method for parallel simulation of port-Hamiltonian systems, ensuring passivity and offering an adaptive accuracy-compute trade-off. It's validated on a coupled-oscillator benchmark.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种能量安全、可提前终止的迭代耦合方法，用于港口-哈密顿系统的并行仿真，确保了被动性并提供了自适应的精度-计算权衡。在耦合振荡器基准上进行了验证。
summary_zh: 大型机器人系统的并行仿真与控制通常依赖于分块时间步进法，然而，有限次迭代耦合可能会因违反功率一致性而注入虚假能量，即使每个子系统都是无源的。 本文提出了一种新的能量安全、可提前终止的迭代耦合方法，用于端口哈密顿子系统，该方法将Douglas--Rachford (DR) 分裂方案嵌入到散射（波）坐标中。 无损互连被强制为波域中的正交约束，而每个子系统贡献一个由其单步积分器引起的离散时间散射端口映射。 在子系统时间步长的离散无源条件下以及一个温和的阻抗调谐条件下，我们证明了一个增强的存储不等式，该不等式证明了耦合宏步在任何有限内部迭代预算下的离散无源性，剩余的失配由一个显式残差捕获。 随着内部预算的增加，分块更新收敛到由相同积分器引起的整体离散时间更新，从而产生一个原则性的、自适应的精度 - 计算权衡，支持在不同计算预算下进行能量一致的实时并行仿真。 在耦合振荡器基准上的实验验证了数值舍入（双精度下约为 10e-14 数量级）时的无源性证书，并表明报告的 RMS 状态误差随着内部迭代预算的增加而单调递减，与硬耦合极限一致。

title: Compact Optical Single-axis Joint Torque Sensor Using Redundant Photo-Reflectors and Quadratic-Programming Calibration
summary: This study proposes a non-contact photo-reflector-based joint torque sensor for precise joint-level torque control and safe physical interaction. Current-sensor-based torque estimation in many collaborative robots suffers from poor low-torque accuracy due to gearbox stiction/friction and current-torque nonlinearity, especially near static conditions. The proposed sensor optically measures micro-deformation of an elastic structure and employs a redundant array of photo-reflectors arranged in four directions to improve sensitivity and signal-to-noise ratio. We further present a quadratic-programming-based calibration method that exploits redundancy to suppress noise and enhance resolution compared to least-squares calibration. The sensor is implemented in a compact form factor (96 mm diameter, 12 mm thickness). Experiments demonstrate a maximum error of 0.083%FS and an RMS error of 0.0266 Nm for z-axis torque measurement. Calibration tests show that the proposed calibration achieves a 3 sigma resolution of 0.0224 Nm at 1 kHz without filtering, corresponding to a 2.14 times improvement over the least-squares baseline. Temperature chamber characterization and rational fitting based compensation mitigate zero drift induced by MCU self heating and motor heat. Motor-level validation via torque control and admittance control confirms improved low torque tracking and disturbance robustness relative to current-sensor-based control.
tldr: This paper introduces a compact, high-precision optical torque sensor for robotic joints, utilizing redundant photo-reflectors and quadratic-programming calibration to improve low-torque accuracy and robustness compared to current-sensor-based methods.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种紧凑型高精度光学扭矩传感器，用于机器人关节。该传感器利用冗余光反射器和二次规划校准，与基于电流传感器的传统方法相比，提高了低扭矩精度和鲁棒性。
summary_zh: 本研究提出一种基于非接触式光反射器的关节扭矩传感器，用于精确的关节级扭矩控制和安全的人机交互。许多协作机器人中基于电流传感器的扭矩估计，由于齿轮箱的静摩擦/摩擦以及电流-扭矩非线性（尤其是在静态条件下）而导致低扭矩精度较差。所提出的传感器通过光学方法测量弹性结构的微变形，并采用在四个方向上排列的冗余光反射器阵列来提高灵敏度和信噪比。我们进一步提出了一种基于二次规划的标定方法，该方法利用冗余来抑制噪声并提高分辨率，与最小二乘法标定相比。该传感器以紧凑的外形实现（直径 96 毫米，厚度 12 毫米）。实验表明，z 轴扭矩测量的最大误差为 0.083%FS，均方根误差为 0.0266 Nm。标定测试表明，所提出的标定方法在 1 kHz 时无需滤波即可实现 0.0224 Nm 的 3 sigma 分辨率，相当于比最小二乘基线提高 2.14 倍。温度箱表征和基于有理函数拟合的补偿减轻了由 MCU 自发热和电机热量引起的零点漂移。通过扭矩控制和导纳控制进行的电机级验证证实，相对于基于电流传感器的控制，低扭矩跟踪和抗扰动能力有所提高。

title: Resilience Meets Autonomy: Governing Embodied AI in Critical Infrastructure
summary: Critical infrastructure increasingly incorporates embodied AI for monitoring, predictive maintenance, and decision support. However, AI systems designed to handle statistically representable uncertainty struggle with cascading failures and crisis dynamics that exceed their training assumptions. This paper argues that Embodied AIs resilience depends on bounded autonomy within a hybrid governance architecture. We outline four oversight modes and map them to critical infrastructure sectors based on task complexity, risk level, and consequence severity. Drawing on the EU AI Act, ISO safety standards, and crisis management research, we argue that effective governance requires a structured allocation of machine capability and human judgement.
tldr: This paper discusses the governance of embodied AI in critical infrastructure, arguing for bounded autonomy within a hybrid architecture to enhance resilience against cascading failures and unexpected events. It proposes oversight modes based on task complexity, risk, and severity, drawing from the EU AI Act and safety standards.
tldr_zh: 本文探讨了关键基础设施中具身人工智能的治理，主张在混合架构中实施有界限的自主性，以增强对级联故障和意外事件的抵御能力。它基于任务复杂性、风险和严重程度，提出了监督模式，并借鉴了欧盟人工智能法案和安全标准。
summary_zh:
摘要：
关键基础设施越来越多地采用具身人工智能进行监控、预测性维护和决策支持。然而，旨在处理统计上可表示的不确定性的人工智能系统，在应对超出其训练假设的级联故障和危机动态时表现不佳。本文认为，具身人工智能的韧性取决于混合治理架构内的有限自主性。我们概述了四种监督模式，并根据任务复杂性、风险等级和后果严重程度将其映射到关键基础设施 sector。借鉴欧盟人工智能法案、ISO安全标准和危机管理研究，我们认为有效的治理需要对机器能力和人类判断进行结构化的分配。

title: Data-driven generalized perimeter control: Zürich case study
summary: Urban traffic congestion is a key challenge for the development of modern cities, requiring advanced control techniques to optimize existing infrastructures usage. Despite the extensive availability of data, modeling such complex systems remains an expensive and time consuming step when designing model-based control approaches. On the other hand, machine learning approaches require simulations to bootstrap models, or are unable to deal with the sparse nature of traffic data and enforce hard constraints. We propose a novel formulation of traffic dynamics based on behavioral systems theory and apply data-enabled predictive control to steer traffic dynamics via dynamic traffic light control. A high-fidelity simulation of the city of Zürich, the largest closed-loop microscopic simulation of urban traffic in the literature to the best of our knowledge, is used to validate the performance of the proposed method in terms of total travel time and CO2 emissions.
tldr: This paper presents a data-driven predictive control method for urban traffic light control, validated on a large-scale simulation of Zurich, aiming to optimize traffic flow and reduce CO2 emissions. It addresses the limitations of both model-based and traditional machine learning approaches.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种数据驱动的预测控制方法，用于城市交通信号灯控制，并在苏黎世的大规模仿真中进行了验证，旨在优化交通流量并减少二氧化碳排放。它解决了基于模型和传统机器学习方法的局限性。
summary_zh: 城市交通拥堵是现代城市发展面临的关键挑战，需要先进的控制技术来优化现有基础设施的利用。尽管数据已经大量可用，但在设计基于模型的控制方法时，对如此复杂的系统进行建模仍然是一个昂贵且耗时的步骤。另一方面，机器学习方法需要模拟来引导模型，或者无法处理交通数据的稀疏性并强制执行硬约束。我们提出了一种基于行为系统理论的交通动力学新型公式，并应用数据驱动的预测控制，通过动态交通信号灯控制来引导交通动态。我们使用苏黎世市的高保真模拟，据我们所知，这是文献中最大的城市交通闭环微观模拟，以总行程时间和二氧化碳排放量来验证所提出方法的性能。

title: FSMC-Pose: Frequency and Spatial Fusion with Multiscale Self-calibration for Cattle Mounting Pose Estimation
summary: Mounting posture is an important visual indicator of estrus in dairy cattle. However, achieving reliable mounting pose estimation in real-world environments remains challenging due to cluttered backgrounds and frequent inter-animal occlusion. We present FSMC-Pose, a top-down framework that integrates a lightweight frequency-spatial fusion backbone, CattleMountNet, and a multiscale self-calibration head, SC2Head. Specifically, we design two algorithmic components for CattleMountNet: the Spatial Frequency Enhancement Block (SFEBlock) and the Receptive Aggregation Block (RABlock). SFEBlock separates cattle from cluttered backgrounds, while RABlock captures multiscale contextual information. The Spatial-Channel Self-Calibration Head (SC2Head) attends to spatial and channel dependencies and introduces a self-calibration branch to mitigate structural misalignment under inter-animal overlap. We construct a mounting dataset, MOUNT-Cattle, covering 1176 mounting instances, which follows the COCO format and supports drop-in training across pose estimation models. Using a comprehensive dataset that combines MOUNT-Cattle with the public NWAFU-Cattle dataset, FSMC-Pose achieves higher accuracy than strong baselines, with markedly lower computational and parameter costs, while maintaining real-time inference on commodity GPUs. Extensive experiments and qualitative analyses show that FSMC-Pose effectively captures and estimates cattle mounting pose in complex and cluttered environments. Dataset and code are available at https://github.com/elianafang/FSMC-Pose.
tldr: The paper introduces FSMC-Pose, a new approach for cattle mounting pose estimation using frequency and spatial fusion with multiscale self-calibration, achieving higher accuracy and efficiency compared to baselines, and releases a new dataset MOUNT-Cattle.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了FSMC-Pose，一种新的牛只骑跨姿势估计方法，使用频率和空间融合以及多尺度自校准，与基线相比实现了更高的准确性和效率，并发布了一个新的数据集MOUNT-Cattle。
summary_zh:
摘要：
站立姿势是奶牛发情的一个重要视觉指标。然而，在真实环境中实现可靠的站立姿势估计仍然充满挑战，这归因于杂乱的背景和频繁的动物间遮挡。我们提出了FSMC-Pose，一个自顶向下的框架，它集成了轻量级的频域-空间融合骨干网络CattleMountNet和一个多尺度自校准头部SC2Head。具体来说，我们为CattleMountNet设计了两个算法组件：空间频域增强模块（SFEBlock）和感受野聚合模块（RABlock）。SFEBlock将牛从杂乱的背景中分离出来，而RABlock捕捉多尺度的上下文信息。空间-通道自校准头部（SC2Head）关注空间和通道的依赖性，并引入一个自校准分支来缓解动物间重叠下的结构性错位。我们构建了一个站立数据集MOUNT-Cattle，涵盖1176个站立实例，该数据集遵循COCO格式，并支持跨姿势估计模型的即插即用训练。使用一个包含MOUNT-Cattle和公开NWAFU-Cattle数据集的综合数据集，FSMC-Pose实现了比强基线更高的精度，同时显著降低了计算和参数成本，并在通用GPU上保持了实时推理。广泛的实验和定性分析表明，FSMC-Pose可以有效地捕获和估计复杂和杂乱环境中奶牛的站立姿势。数据集和代码可在https://github.com/elianafang/FSMC-Pose获取。

title: Surrogate-Assisted Genetic Programming with Rank-Based Phenotypic Characterisation for Dynamic Multi-Mode Project Scheduling
summary:
The dynamic multi-mode resource-constrained project scheduling problem (DMRCPSP) is of practical importance, as it requires making real-time decisions under changing project states and resource availability. Genetic Programming (GP) has been shown to effectively evolve heuristic rules for such decision-making tasks; however, the evolutionary process typically relies on a large number of simulation-based fitness evaluations, resulting in high computational cost. Surrogate models offer a promising solution to reduce evaluation cost, but their application to GP requires problem-specific phenotypic characterisation (PC) schemes of heuristic rules. There is currently a lack of suitable PC schemes for GP applied to DMRCPSP.
  This paper proposes a rank-based PC scheme derived from heuristic-driven ordering of eligible activity-mode pairs and activity groups in decision situations. The resulting PC vectors enable a surrogate model to estimate the fitness of unevaluated GP individuals. Based on this scheme, a surrogate-assisted GP algorithm is developed. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed surrogate-assisted GP can identify high-quality heuristic rules consistently earlier than the state-of-the-art GP approach for DMRCPSP, while introducing only marginal computational overhead. Further analyses demonstrate that the surrogate model provides useful guidance for offspring selection, leading to improved evolutionary efficiency.
tldr: This paper introduces a surrogate-assisted genetic programming (GP) approach with a novel rank-based phenotypic characterisation scheme for solving dynamic multi-mode resource-constrained project scheduling problems (DMRCPSP). The method demonstrates improved efficiency over standard GP in finding high-quality heuristic rules.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种带有新型基于排序的表型特征方案的代理辅助遗传规划(GP)方法，用于解决动态多模式资源约束项目调度问题(DMRCPSP)。实验表明，该方法在寻找高质量启发式规则方面比标准GP更有效。
summary_zh:
动态多模式资源受限项目调度问题 (DMRCPSP) 具有重要的实际意义，因为它需要在不断变化的项目状态和资源可用性的情况下进行实时决策。遗传编程 (GP) 已被证明可以有效地演化出用于此类决策任务的启发式规则；然而，进化过程通常依赖于大量基于仿真的适应度评估，从而导致较高的计算成本。代理模型为降低评估成本提供了一种有前景的解决方案，但其在 GP 中的应用需要针对问题的特定启发式规则表型特征化 (PC) 方案。目前，针对应用于 DMRCPSP 的 GP，缺乏合适的 PC 方案。

本文提出了一种基于排序的 PC 方案，该方案源自决策情境中合格的活动-模式对和活动组的启发式驱动排序。由此产生的 PC 向量使代理模型能够估计未评估的 GP 个体的适应度。基于此方案，开发了一种代理辅助 GP 算法。实验结果表明，与最先进的 DMRCPSP 的 GP 方法相比，所提出的代理辅助 GP 能够始终如一地更早识别出高质量的启发式规则，同时仅引入边际计算开销。进一步的分析表明，代理模型为后代选择提供了有用的指导，从而提高了进化效率。

title: From Workflow Automation to Capability Closure: A Formal Framework for Safe and Revenue-Aware Customer Service AI
summary: Customer service automation is undergoing a structural transformation. The dominant paradigm is shifting from scripted chatbots and single-agent responders toward networks of specialised AI agents that compose capabilities dynamically across billing, service provision, payments, and fulfilment. This shift introduces a safety gap that no current platform has closed: two agents individually verified as safe can, when combined, reach a forbidden goal through an emergent conjunctive dependency that neither possesses alone.
tldr: This paper introduces a formal framework for safe and revenue-aware customer service AI, addressing the emergent safety risks arising from composed AI agents in billing, service, payment, and fulfillment.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一个安全且感知收益的客户服务AI的形式化框架，旨在解决由账单、服务、支付和履约中组合的AI代理所产生的突发安全风险。
summary_zh: 客户服务自动化正在经历结构性转变。主导范式正从脚本化的聊天机器人和单代理响应器转变为专门的AI代理网络，这些代理在计费、服务提供、支付和履行等方面动态组合能力。这种转变引入了一个安全缺口，目前的任何平台都没有弥合：即使单独验证为安全的两个代理，在组合后也能通过它们单独不具备的新兴连接依赖关系，共同达到一个被禁止的目标。

title: Learning Human-Object Interaction for 3D Human Pose Estimation from LiDAR Point Clouds
summary: Understanding humans from LiDAR point clouds is one of the most critical tasks in autonomous driving due to its close relationships with pedestrian safety, yet it remains challenging in the presence of diverse human-object interactions and cluttered backgrounds. Nevertheless, existing methods largely overlook the potential of leveraging human-object interactions to build robust 3D human pose estimation frameworks. There are two major challenges that motivate the incorporation of human-object interaction. First, human-object interactions introduce spatial ambiguity between human and object points, which often leads to erroneous 3D human keypoint predictions in interaction regions. Second, there exists severe class imbalance in the number of points between interacting and non-interacting body parts, with the interaction-frequent regions such as hand and foot being sparsely observed in LiDAR data. To address these challenges, we propose a Human-Object Interaction Learning (HOIL) framework for robust 3D human pose estimation from LiDAR point clouds. To mitigate the spatial ambiguity issue, we present human-object interaction-aware contrastive learning (HOICL) that effectively enhances feature discrimination between human and object points, particularly in interaction regions. To alleviate the class imbalance issue, we introduce contact-aware part-guided pooling (CPPool) that adaptively reallocates representational capacity by compressing overrepresented points while preserving informative points from interacting body parts. In addition, we present an optional contact-based temporal refinement that refines erroneous per-frame keypoint estimates using contact cues over time. As a result, our HOIL effectively leverages human-object interaction to resolve spatial ambiguity and class imbalance in interaction regions. Codes will be released.
tldr: This paper introduces a Human-Object Interaction Learning (HOIL) framework to improve 3D human pose estimation from LiDAR point clouds by addressing spatial ambiguity and class imbalance in interaction regions using contrastive learning and part-guided pooling.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种人-物交互学习（HOIL）框架，通过使用对比学习和部分引导池化来解决交互区域中的空间模糊和类别不平衡问题，从而提高从LiDAR点云中进行3D人体姿势估计的准确性。
summary_zh:
摘要：
从激光雷达点云中理解人类是自动驾驶中最关键的任务之一，因为它与行人安全密切相关，但在存在多样化的人-物交互和杂乱背景的情况下，这项任务仍然具有挑战性。然而，现有方法在很大程度上忽略了利用人-物交互构建鲁棒的3D人体姿态估计框架的潜力。融入人-物交互的动机源于两个主要挑战。首先，人-物交互引入了人体和物体点之间的空间模糊性，这常常导致交互区域中错误的3D人体关键点预测。其次，交互和非交互身体部位之间的点数量存在严重的类别不平衡，在激光雷达数据中，诸如手和脚等交互频繁区域的观测稀疏。 为了解决这些挑战，我们提出了一个人-物交互学习（HOIL）框架，用于从激光雷达点云中进行鲁棒的3D人体姿态估计。为了缓解空间模糊问题，我们提出了一种人-物交互感知的对比学习（HOICL），它有效地增强了人体和物体点之间，特别是在交互区域中的特征辨别能力。 为了缓解类别不平衡问题，我们引入了接触感知的部件引导池化（CPPool），它通过压缩过度表示的点，同时保留来自交互身体部位的信息点，自适应地重新分配表征能力。此外，我们提出了一个可选的基于接触的时间细化模块，该模块利用随时间推移的接触线索来细化错误的每帧关键点估计。因此，我们的HOIL有效地利用人-物交互来解决交互区域中的空间模糊性和类别不平衡问题。代码将会开源。

title: NanoGS: Training-Free Gaussian Splat Simplification
summary: 3D Gaussian Splat (3DGS) enables high-fidelity, real-time novel view synthesis by representing scenes with large sets of anisotropic primitives, but often requires millions of Splats, incurring significant storage and transmission costs. Most existing compression methods rely on GPU-intensive post-training optimization with calibrated images, limiting practical deployment. We introduce NanoGS, a training-free and lightweight framework for Gaussian Splat simplification. Instead of relying on image-based rendering supervision, NanoGS formulates simplification as local pairwise merging over a sparse spatial graph. The method approximates a pair of Gaussians with a single primitive using mass preserved moment matching and evaluates merge quality through a principled merge cost between the original mixture and its approximation. By restricting merge candidates to local neighborhoods and selecting compatible pairs efficiently, NanoGS produces compact Gaussian representations while preserving scene structure and appearance. NanoGS operates directly on existing Gaussian Splat models, runs efficiently on CPU, and preserves the standard 3DGS parameterization, enabling seamless integration with existing rendering pipelines. Experiments demonstrate that NanoGS substantially reduces primitive count while maintaining high rendering fidelity, providing an efficient and practical solution for Gaussian Splat simplification. Our project website is available at https://saliteta.github.io/NanoGS/.
tldr: NanoGS is a training-free, CPU-efficient method for simplifying 3D Gaussian Splat representations by local pairwise merging, significantly reducing the number of primitives while preserving rendering fidelity.
tldr_zh: NanoGS是一种无需训练、CPU高效的方法，通过局部成对合并来简化3D高斯溅射表示，从而在保持渲染逼真度的同时，显著减少了图元的数量。
summary_zh: 3D高斯溅射(3DGS)通过使用大量各向异性基元表示场景，实现了高保真、实时的 novel view synthesis，但通常需要数百万个Splats，从而导致显著的存储和传输成本。大多数现有的压缩方法依赖于GPU密集型的后训练优化以及校准图像，限制了实际部署。我们提出了NanoGS，一个无需训练且轻量级的高斯溅射简化框架。 NanoGS没有依赖于基于图像的渲染监督，而是将简化建模为稀疏空间图上的局部两两合并。该方法利用质量守恒的矩匹配用单个基元近似一对高斯分布，并通过原始混合与其近似之间的原则性合并成本来评估合并质量。通过将合并候选限制在局部邻域并有效地选择兼容的pair，NanoGS生成紧凑的高斯表示，同时保留场景结构和外观。 NanoGS直接在现有的高斯溅射模型上运行，在CPU上高效运行，并保留标准3DGS参数化，从而与现有的渲染管道无缝集成。实验表明，NanoGS在保持高渲染保真度的同时显著减少了基元数量，为高斯溅射简化提供了一种高效实用的解决方案。我们的项目网站位于https://saliteta.github.io/NanoGS/。

title: Long-Horizon Traffic Forecasting via Incident-Aware Conformal Spatio-Temporal Transformers
summary: Reliable multi-horizon traffic forecasting is challenging because network conditions are stochastic, incident disruptions are intermittent, and effective spatial dependencies vary across time-of-day patterns. This study is conducted on the Ohio Department of Transportation (ODOT) traffic count data and corresponding ODOT crash records. This work utilizes a Spatio-Temporal Transformer (STT) model with Adaptive Conformal Prediction (ACP) to produce multi-horizon forecasts with calibrated uncertainty. We propose a piecewise Coefficient of Variation (CV) strategy that models hour-to-hour traveltime variability using a log-normal distribution, enabling the construction of a per-hour dynamic adjacency matrix. We further perturb edge weights using incident-related severity signals derived from the ODOT crash dataset that comprises incident clearance time, weather conditions, speed violations, work zones, and roadway functional class, to capture localized disruptions and peak/off-peak transitions. This dynamic graph construction replaces a fixed-CV assumption and better represents changing traffic conditions within the forecast window. For validation, we generate extended trips via multi-hour loop runs on the Columbus, Ohio, network in SUMO simulations and apply a Monte Carlo simulation to obtain travel-time distributions for a Vehicle Under Test (VUT). Experiments demonstrate improved long-horizon accuracy and well-calibrated prediction intervals compared to other baseline methods.
tldr: This paper presents a novel incident-aware Spatio-Temporal Transformer (STT) model with Adaptive Conformal Prediction (ACP) for long-horizon traffic forecasting, incorporating dynamic graph construction and incident-related severity signals to improve accuracy and uncertainty calibration.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种新颖的基于事件感知的时空Transformer模型(STT)与自适应共形预测(ACP)相结合的长程交通预测方法。该方法融合了动态图构建和与事件相关的严重程度信号，以提高预测精度和不确定性校准。
summary_zh: 可靠的多时域交通流量预测极具挑战性，因为网络状况具有随机性、事故扰动是间歇性的，并且有效的空间依赖性会随每日不同时段的模式而变化。本研究基于俄亥俄州交通部 (ODOT) 的交通流量计数数据和相应的ODOT事故记录展开。本研究采用带有自适应一致性预测 (ACP) 的时空Transformer (STT) 模型，生成带有校准不确定性的多时域预测。我们提出了一种分段变异系数 (CV) 策略，该策略使用对数正态分布来建模每小时的行程时间可变性，从而能够构建一个逐小时的动态邻接矩阵。我们进一步使用源自ODOT事故数据集的与事故相关的严重程度信号来扰动边权重，该数据集包括事故清理时间、天气状况、超速违章、施工区域和道路功能等级，以捕获局部扰动和高峰/非高峰过渡。这种动态图构建取代了固定的CV假设，并能更好地表示预测窗口内不断变化的交通状况。为了进行验证，我们在SUMO仿真中的俄亥俄州哥伦布网络上，通过多小时循环运行生成扩展行程，并应用蒙特卡罗模拟来获得被测车辆 (VUT) 的行程时间分布。实验表明，与其他基线方法相比，该方法提高了长时域预测的准确性，并获得了良好校准的预测区间。

title: Physics-integrated neural differentiable modeling for immersed boundary systems
summary: Accurately, efficiently, and stably computing complex fluid flows and their evolution near solid boundaries over long horizons remains challenging. Conventional numerical solvers require fine grids and small time steps to resolve near-wall dynamics, resulting in high computational costs, while purely data-driven surrogate models accumulate rollout errors and lack robustness under extrapolative conditions. To address these issues, this study extends existing neural PDE solvers by developing a physics-integrated differentiable framework for long-horizon prediction of immersed-boundary flows. A key design aspect of the framework includes an important improvement, namely the structural integration of physical principles into an end-to-end differentiable architecture incorporating a PDE-based intermediate velocity module and a multi-direct forcing immersed boundary module, both adhering to the pressure-projection procedure for incompressible flow computation. The computationally expensive pressure projection step is substituted with a learned implicit correction using ConvResNet blocks to reduce cost, and a sub-iteration strategy is introduced to separate the embedded physics module's stability requirement from the surrogate model's time step, enabling stable coarse-grid autoregressive rollouts with large effective time increments. The framework uses only single-step supervision for training, eliminating long-horizon backpropagation and reducing training time to under one hour on a single GPU. Evaluations on benchmark cases of flow past a stationary cylinder and a rotationally oscillating cylinder at Re=100 show the proposed model consistently outperforms purely data-driven, physics-loss-constrained, and coarse-grid numerical baselines in flow-field fidelity and long-horizon stability, while achieving an approximately 200-fold inference speedup over the high-resolution solver.
tldr: This paper introduces a physics-integrated neural differentiable framework for long-horizon prediction of immersed-boundary flows, achieving significant speedups and improved stability compared to traditional and purely data-driven methods.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种物理集成神经可微框架，用于对浸没边界流进行长时程预测，与传统方法和纯数据驱动方法相比，实现了显著的加速和更高的稳定性。
summary_zh: 准确、高效且稳定地计算复杂流体流动及其在固体边界附近的长时程演化仍然具有挑战性。传统的数值求解器需要精细网格和较小的时间步长来解析近壁动力学，导致极高的计算成本，而纯粹的数据驱动代理模型会累积滚动误差，并且在需要外推的情况下缺乏鲁棒性。为了解决这些问题，本研究通过开发一种物理集成可微框架来扩展现有的神经偏微分方程求解器，用于浸没边界流动的长时程预测。 该框架的一个关键设计方面包括一个重要的改进，即：将物理原理结构性地集成到端到端可微架构中，其中包含基于偏微分方程的中间速度模块和多重直接力浸没边界模块，两者都遵循不可压缩流计算的压力投影过程。 计算成本高昂的压力投影步骤被使用ConvResNet块学习到的隐式校正所取代，以降低成本，并且引入了子迭代策略，以将嵌入式物理模块的稳定性要求与代理模型的时间步长分离，从而实现以大有效时间增量进行稳定的粗网格自回归滚动。 该框架仅使用单步监督进行训练，消除了长时程反向传播，并将训练时间缩短到单个GPU上的一小时以内。 在雷诺数Re=100下，对流过静止圆柱体和旋转振荡圆柱体的基准案例的评估表明，所提出的模型在流场保真度和长时程稳定性方面始终优于纯粹的数据驱动、物理损失约束和粗网格数值基线，同时实现了相对于高分辨率求解器约200倍的推理加速。

title: Minimum-Action Learning: Energy-Constrained Symbolic Model Selection for Physical Law Identification from Noisy Data
summary: Identifying physical laws from noisy observational data is a central challenge in scientific machine learning. We present Minimum-Action Learning (MAL), a framework that selects symbolic force laws from a pre-specified basis library by minimizing a Triple-Action functional combining trajectory reconstruction, architectural sparsity, and energy-conservation enforcement. A wide-stencil acceleration-matching technique reduces noise variance by 10,000x, transforming an intractable problem (SNR ~0.02) into a learnable one (SNR ~1.6); this preprocessing is the critical enabler shared by all methods tested, including SINDy variants. On two benchmarks -- Kepler gravity and Hooke's law -- MAL recovers the correct force law with Kepler exponent p = 3.01 +/- 0.01 at ~0.07 kWh (40% reduction vs. prediction-error-only baselines). The raw correct-basis rate is 40% for Kepler and 90% for Hooke; an energy-conservation-based criterion discriminates the true force law in all cases, yielding 100% pipeline-level identification. Basis library sensitivity experiments show that near-confounders degrade selection (20% with added r^{-2.5} and r^{-1.5}), while distant additions are harmless, and the conservation diagnostic remains informative even when the correct basis is absent. Direct comparison with noise-robust SINDy variants, Hamiltonian Neural Networks, and Lagrangian Neural Networks confirms MAL's distinct niche: interpretable, energy-constrained model selection that combines symbolic basis identification with dynamical rollout validation.
tldr: The paper introduces Minimum-Action Learning (MAL), a method for identifying physical laws from noisy data by minimizing a Triple-Action functional that combines trajectory reconstruction, sparsity, and energy conservation. It demonstrates superior performance compared to SINDy, Hamiltonian Neural Networks, and Lagrangian Neural Networks in terms of interpretability and energy constraint.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了最小作用量学习 (MAL)，这是一种通过最小化结合轨迹重建、稀疏性和能量守恒的三重作用泛函，从噪声数据中识别物理定律的方法。它在可解释性和能量约束方面，展示了优于 SINDy、哈密顿神经网络和拉格朗日神经网络的性能。
summary_zh: 从含噪声观测数据中识别物理定律是科学机器学习中的一个核心挑战。我们提出了最小作用量学习(MAL)框架，该框架通过最小化一个三重作用量泛函，从预先指定的基函数库中选择符合物理定律的符号力函数。该泛函结合了轨迹重构、架构稀疏性和能量守恒约束。一种宽模板加速度匹配技术将噪声方差降低了10,000倍，将一个难以处理的问题（信噪比约为0.02）转化为一个可学习的问题（信噪比约为1.6）；这种预处理是所有测试方法（包括SINDy变体）的关键推动因素。在两个基准测试——开普勒引力和胡克定律上，MAL以~0.07千瓦时(比仅基于预测误差的基线方法减少40%)恢复了正确的力定律，开普勒指数为p = 3.01 +/- 0.01。开普勒定律和胡克定律的原始正确基函数率分别为40%和90%；一个基于能量守恒的准则在所有情况下都能区分真实的力定律，从而实现100%的流程级识别。基函数库敏感性实验表明，近混杂项会降低选择性能（添加r^{-2.5}和r^{-1.5}时降低20%），而远距离添加则无害，即使在缺少正确的基函数时，守恒诊断仍然有效。与抗噪声SINDy变体、哈密顿神经网络和拉格朗日神经网络的直接比较证实了MAL的独特优势：可解释的、能量约束的模型选择，将符号基函数识别与动态展开验证相结合。

title: SE(3)-LIO: Smooth IMU Propagation With Jointly Distributed Poses on SE(3) Manifold for Accurate and Robust LiDAR-Inertial Odometry
summary: In estimating odometry accurately, an inertial measurement unit (IMU) is widely used owing to its high-rate measurements, which can be utilized to obtain motion information through IMU propagation. In this paper, we address the limitations of existing IMU propagation methods in terms of motion prediction and motion compensation. In motion prediction, the existing methods typically represent a 6-DoF pose by separating rotation and translation and propagate them on their respective manifold, so that the rotational variation is not effectively incorporated into translation propagation. During motion compensation, the relative transformation between predicted poses is used to compensate motion-induced distortion in other measurements, while inherent errors in the predicted poses introduce uncertainty in the relative transformation. To tackle these challenges, we represent and propagate the pose on SE(3) manifold, where propagated translation properly accounts for rotational variation. Furthermore, we precisely characterize the relative transformation uncertainty by considering the correlation between predicted poses, and incorporate this uncertainty into the measurement noise during motion compensation. To this end, we propose a LiDAR-inertial odometry (LIO), referred to as SE(3)-LIO, that integrates the proposed IMU propagation and uncertainty-aware motion compensation (UAMC). We validate the effectiveness of SE(3)-LIO on diverse datasets. Our source code and additional material are available at: https://se3-lio.github.io/.
tldr: This paper introduces SE(3)-LIO, a LiDAR-inertial odometry system that improves IMU propagation by representing poses on the SE(3) manifold and uses uncertainty-aware motion compensation, leading to more accurate and robust odometry. It addresses limitations in existing IMU propagation methods related to motion prediction and compensation.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了SE(3)-LIO，一种激光雷达惯性里程计系统，通过在SE(3)流形上表示姿态并使用感知不确定性的运动补偿来改进IMU传播，从而实现更准确和鲁棒的里程计。它解决了现有IMU传播方法在运动预测和补偿方面的局限性。
summary_zh: 在精确估计里程计时，惯性测量单元 (IMU) 因其高频率测量而得到广泛应用，这些测量可用于通过 IMU 传播来获取运动信息。本文旨在解决现有 IMU 传播方法在运动预测和运动补偿方面的局限性。在运动预测方面，现有方法通常通过分离旋转和平移来表示 6 自由度 (6-DoF) 姿态，并在各自流形上进行传播，导致旋转变化未能有效地纳入平移传播中。在运动补偿方面，预测姿态之间的相对变换用于补偿其他测量中运动引起的畸变，而预测姿态中的固有误差会在相对变换中引入不确定性。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出在 SE(3) 流形上表示和传播姿态，其中传播的平移可以正确地考虑旋转变化。此外，我们通过考虑预测姿态之间的相关性来精确地表征相对变换的不确定性，并将这种不确定性纳入到运动补偿期间的测量噪声中。为此，我们提出了一种 LiDAR-惯性里程计 (LIO)，称为 SE(3)-LIO，它集成了提出的 IMU 传播和感知不确定性的运动补偿 (UAMC)。我们在各种数据集上验证了 SE(3)-LIO 的有效性。我们的源代码和其他资料可在以下网址获取：https://se3-lio.github.io/。

title: Efficient Brood Cell Detection in Layer Trap Nests for Bees and Wasps: Balancing Labeling Effort and Species Coverage
summary: Monitoring cavity-nesting wild bees and wasps is vital for biodiversity research and conservation. Layer trap nests (LTNs) are emerging as a valuable tool to study the abundance and species richness of these insects, offering insights into their nesting activities and ecological needs. However, manually evaluating LTNs to detect and classify brood cells is labor-intensive and time-consuming. To address this, we propose a deep learning based approach for efficient brood cell detection and classification in LTNs. LTNs present additional challenges due to densely packed brood cells, leading to a high labeling effort per image. Moreover, we observe a significant imbalance in class distribution, with common species having notably more occurrences than rare species. Comprehensive labeling of common species is time-consuming and exacerbates data imbalance, while partial labeling introduces data incompleteness which degrades model performance. To reduce labeling effort and mitigate the impact of unlabeled data, we introduce a novel Constrained False Positive Loss (CFPL) strategy. CFPL dynamically masks predictions from unlabeled data, preventing them from interfering with the classification loss during training. We evaluate our approach on a dataset of 712 LTN images collected over one season, covering 28 fine-grained classes describing the taxonomy and status of brood cells. To minimize labeling effort, we limit the training set to a maximum of 300 labels per class. Experimental results demonstrate that deep learning can be effectively used to detect brood cells in LTNs. Our CFPL method further improves performance and balances model accuracy and labeling effort while also mitigating class imbalance.
tldr: This paper proposes a deep learning approach with a novel loss function (CFPL) to efficiently detect and classify brood cells in layer trap nests, addressing challenges of dense packing, class imbalance, and high labeling effort.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种深度学习方法，结合一种新颖的损失函数（CFPL），以高效地检测和分类层叠陷阱巢中的蜂巢单元，解决了密集堆叠、类别不平衡和高标注成本的挑战。
summary_zh: 监测在洞巢筑巢的野生蜂和黄蜂对生物多样性研究和保护至关重要。层状巢陷阱（LTNs）正逐渐成为一种研究这些昆虫丰度和物种丰富度的宝贵工具，为它们的筑巢活动和生态需求提供见解。然而，手动评估LTNs以检测和分类育雏室劳动强度大且耗时。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种基于深度学习的方法，用于高效地检测和分类LTNs中的育雏室。由于育雏室密集堆积，LTNs带来了额外的挑战，导致每个图像的标注工作量很大。此外，我们观察到类分布存在显著的不平衡，常见物种的出现次数明显多于稀有物种。对常见物种进行全面标注既耗时又加剧了数据不平衡，而部分标注则会引入数据不完整性，从而降低模型性能。为了减少标注工作量并减轻未标注数据的影响，我们引入了一种新的约束假阳性损失（CFPL）策略。CFPL动态地屏蔽来自未标注数据的预测结果，防止它们干扰训练期间的分类损失。 我们在一个超过一个季度收集的包含712张LTN图像的数据集上评估了我们的方法，其中涵盖了描述育雏室分类学和状态的28个细粒度类别。为了最大限度地减少标注工作量，我们将训练集限制为每个类最多300个标签。实验结果表明，深度学习可有效地用于检测LTNs中的育雏室。我们的CFPL方法进一步提高了性能，并在平衡模型准确性和标注工作量的同时缓解了类不平衡问题。

title: Data-driven forced response analysis with min-max representations of nonlinear restoring forces
summary: This paper discusses a novel data-driven nonlinearity identification method for mechanical systems with nonlinear restoring forces such as polynomial, piecewise-linear, and general displacement-dependent nonlinearities. The proposed method is built upon the universal approximation theorem that states that a nonlinear function can be approximated by a linear combination of activation functions in artificial neural network framework. The proposed approach utilizes piecewise linear springs with initial gaps to act as the activation functions of the neurons of artificial neural networks. A library of piecewise linear springs with initial gaps are constructed, and the contributions of the springs on the nonlinear restoring force are determined by solving the linear regression problems. The piecewise linear springs are realized by combinations of min and max functions with biases. The proposed method is applied to a Duffing oscillator with cubic stiffness, and a piecewise linear oscillator with a gap and their nonlinearities are successfully determined from their free responses. The obtained models are then used for conducting forced response analysis and the results match well with those of the original system. The method is then applied to experimentally-obtained free response data of a cantilevered plate that is subjected to magnetic restoring force, and successfully finds the piecewise linear representation of the magnetic force. It is also shown that the obtained model is capable of accurately capturing the steady-state response of the system subject to harmonic base excitation.
tldr: The paper introduces a data-driven method for identifying and representing nonlinear restoring forces in mechanical systems using piecewise linear springs modeled by min-max functions, demonstrating its effectiveness on simulated and experimental data for forced response analysis.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种数据驱动方法，利用由min-max函数建模的分段线性弹簧来识别和表示机械系统中的非线性恢复力，并在模拟和实验数据上验证了其用于强迫响应分析的有效性。
summary_zh: 本文讨论了一种新颖的基于数据的非线性辨识方法，用于辨识具有非线性恢复力的机械系统，例如多项式、分段线性和一般位移相关的非线性。该方法基于通用逼近定理，即非线性函数可以通过人工神经网络框架中激活函数的线性组合来逼近。本文利用带有初始间隙的分段线性弹簧，使其充当人工神经网络神经元的激活函数。构建了一个带有初始间隙的分段线性弹簧库，并通过求解线性回归问题来确定弹簧对非线性恢复力的贡献。分段线性弹簧通过带有偏置的最小值和最大值函数的组合来实现。该方法应用于具有三次刚度的Duffing振荡器和具有间隙的分段线性振荡器，并成功地从它们的自由响应中确定了它们的非线性。然后利用获得的模型进行强迫响应分析，结果与原始系统的结果吻合良好。随后，该方法应用于受磁恢复力作用的悬臂梁的实验自由响应数据，并成功地找到了磁力的分段线性表示。结果还表明，获得的模型能够准确地捕捉到系统在谐波基座激励下的稳态响应。
